findings: Destination Port feature exhibits strong distributional divergence between BENIGN and DDoS classes, with DDoS dominated by a single port and near-zero entropy.
No evidence of narrow structural dependency clusters was found for Destination Port, indicating the divergence is distributional rather than dependency-driven.
An unresolved tension remains about the impact of moderate class imbalance on the stability and generalizability of this distributional signal.
contradictions: Potential confounding effect of class imbalance on distributional divergence remains unresolved.
limitations: Analysis limited to local evidence within e33; broader dataset context not incorporated.
